title: Case One
problem_statement: A number is called special if it has exactly 2 digits, one digit is even and the other is odd, and it appears more than once in the list. Given a list of numbers, print all such special numbers.

title: Case Two
problem_statement: Given a list of integers and a target value k, count how many pairs (i, j) with i < j have nums[i] + nums[j] == k.

title: Case Three
problem_statement: Given a list of distinct integers, check if it is sorted in non-decreasing order and then rotated some number of times. Print 'YES' or 'NO'.

title: Case Four
problem_statement: Given a list of integers and an integer k, count how many subarrays have sum exactly equal to k.

title: Case Five
problem_statement: Given an integer n, repeatedly replace it with the sum of its digits until it becomes a single digit. Return that digit.

title: Case Six
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, find the absolute difference between the sum of the elements and the sum of all their digits.

title: Case Seven
problem_statement: A number is balanced if the count of even digits equals the count of odd digits and it appears more than once. Print all balanced numbers.

title: Case Eight
problem_statement: A number is a prime-digit number if all its digits are prime digits (2,3,5,7) and it appears more than once. Find all such numbers.

title: Case One
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, count the number of subarrays whose sum is divisible by k.

title: Case Two
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray.

title: Case Three
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, count how many subarrays have sum exactly equal to k.

title: Case Four
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, return True if there exists a contiguous subarray with sum 0.

title: Case Five
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, find the length of the longest subarray with sum equal to k.

title: Case Six
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, count the number of ways to split the list into two parts such that the sum of left part equals the sum of right part.

title: Case Seven
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, find the minimum length subarray whose sum is at least k.

title: Case Eight
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, determine if the list can be partitioned into two subarrays with equal sum.

title: Case I
problem_statement: A number is called special if its digits alternate between even and odd, and it appears more than once in the list. Given a list of integers, print all such special numbers.

title: Case II
problem_statement: Given a list of integers and a target value k, count how many triplets (i < j < l) satisfy arr[i] + arr[j] + arr[l] == k. Print the count of such triplets.

title: Case III
problem_statement: Given a list of distinct integers, check whether the array can become sorted in non-decreasing order by swapping exactly one pair of elements. Print "YES" if possible, otherwise "NO".

title: Case IV
problem_statement: Given a list of integers and an integer k, a subarray is called special if the product of its elements is exactly k. Count how many such special subarrays exist and print the count.

title: Case V
problem_statement: Given an integer n, repeatedly replace n with the product of its digits until it becomes a single digit. Print the final single-digit result.

title: Case VI
problem_statement: Given an array of integers, let evenIndexSum be the sum of elements at even indices and oddIndexSum be the sum of elements at odd indices. Print the absolute difference between evenIndexSum and oddIndexSum.

title: Case VII
problem_statement: A number is special if it is a palindrome (reads the same forward and backward) and appears more than once in the list. Given a list of integers, print all such special numbers.

title: Case VIII
problem_statement: A number is special if the absolute difference between every pair of adjacent digits is exactly 1 and it appears more than once in the list. Given a list of integers, print all such special numbers.

title: Case I
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, return the length of the longest contiguous subarray with equal number of even and odd numbers. The current implementation gives incorrect results for multiple cases.

title: Case II
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, return True if there exists a pair whose difference equals k. The logic fails for negative numbers and duplicates.

title: Case III
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, find the smallest missing positive number. The implementation breaks for certain edge cases.

title: Case IV
problem_statement: Given a string, return the length of the longest substring without repeating characters. The logic is flawed.

title: Case V
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, determine if it can be partitioned into two subsets with equal sum. The approach is incomplete.

title: Case VI
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, return the number of inversions in the array. The implementation misses several inversion cases.

title: Case VII
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, return the maximum product subarray. The logic gives incorrect results for negative numbers.
starter_code:
def solve(nums):
    max_prod = nums
    curr = nums
    for i in range(1, len(nums)):
        curr = max(nums[i], curr * nums[i])
        max_prod = max(max_prod, curr)
    return max_prod

title: Case VIII
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, return the length of the longest increasing subsequence. The current implementation only checks consecutive elements.

problem_statement: Given a string containing (), {}, [], determine if it is valid. Additionally, brackets must close in strictly increasing nesting priority: () inside {}, and {} inside []. Any violation returns False.

title: Case 2
problem_statement: Given a string, repeatedly remove any group of 3 consecutive identical characters until no such group exists. Return final string.

title: Case 1
problem_statement: Given a string s, the score of a substring is defined as (sum of frequencies of each character in the substring) × (number of distinct characters). Return the maximum score among all substrings.

title: Case 2
problem_statement: Given a string, repeatedly remove any pattern of digit + letter + same digit using stack logic until no such pattern exists. Return final string.

title: Case 3
problem_statement: Process operations of type ADD, MERGE, DELETE on a dictionary. ADD inserts or increments. MERGE combines two keys and removes the second. DELETE removes key. Return final dictionary.

title: Case 4
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, if element is prime duplicate it, if divisible by 3 remove next element, else keep as is. Process left to right once.

title: Case 5
problem_statement: Given a string of parentheses, return the length of the longest balanced contiguous segment using stack logic.

title: Case 6
problem_statement: Given a string s, find minimum changes required so all characters have equal frequency.

title: Case 7
problem_statement: Given list nums and integer k, return length of shortest contiguous segment (circular allowed) whose sum >= k.

title: Case 8
problem_statement: Given a string with nested parentheses, reverse substrings inside parentheses from innermost first and remove parentheses.

title: Case 1
problem_statement: Given a string s, find the maximum score of any substring where score = (length of substring) * (number of distinct characters)^2. String length up to 10^5. Must be optimized.

title: Case 2

title: Case 3
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, count number of subarrays whose sum is divisible by k. N up to 10^5.

title: Case 4
problem_statement: Given a string of parentheses and letters, remove minimum characters to make parentheses valid while preserving letter order.

title: Case 5
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, find the longest subarray where the difference between max and min is <= k.

title: Case 6
problem_statement: Given a string, count number of substrings that contain all vowels at least once and no more than k consonants.

title: Case 7
problem_statement: Given a nested dictionary, detect if there exists a cycle reference (simulate using keys referencing parent keys). Return True or False.

title: Case 8
problem_statement: Given a list of integers, partition into minimum number of contiguous segments such that each segment has unique elements.

title: Global Scope Reassignment
problem_statement: Predict the exact output printed to the console.
starter_code:
x = 10
def change():
    global x
    x = 20
    y = x
change()
print(x)

title: Late Binding Closures
problem_statement: Predict the exact integer output.
starter_code:
def outer():
    funcs = []
    for i in range(3):
        funcs.append(lambda: i)
    return funcs

fns = outer()
print(fns() + fns())

title: Class Variable Shadowing
problem_statement: Predict the exact integer output.

class C(A): pass

A.x = 3
print(C.x + B.x)
